# AutoAW

> AutoAW automatically discovers optimal multi-agent workflow configurations — co-evolving topology, prompts, models, and tools to hit the Pareto frontier of quality, cost, and latency.

AutoAW is an automated optimization platform for agentic AI workflows. Given a task description or an existing workflow, it uses evolutionary search to find the cheapest configuration that still meets your quality and latency requirements.

## What it does

- Accepts a natural-language task description or an existing workflow (LangGraph, DSPy, custom Python)
- Searches over multi-agent architectures: multi-agent committees and agentic glue + skills
- Co-evolves topology, prompts, model assignments, and tool selections simultaneously
- Evaluates every candidate against a user-supplied eval dataset with user-defined utility weights (quality, cost, latency)
- Returns the Pareto frontier: every non-dominated configuration across all objectives

## Key benchmark results (GAIA, customer-support copilot, 1 400-ticket eval)

- Quality: 74.1% vs 69.2% Claude Sonnet 4.5 baseline (+5.3 pp)
- Cost: $4.20/run vs $26.80 GPT-5 single-agent (6.7× cheaper)
- Latency p50: 2.4 s vs 8.7 s (3.6× faster)
- Time to SOTA: 38 minutes wall-clock, 94 candidates explored

## How it works (four steps)

1. Describe the task or import an existing graph
2. Choose an eval dataset and set utility weights (quality, cost, speed)
3. AutoAW runs a search loop — swapping models, splitting/merging agents, editing prompts, pruning tools
4. Pick any point on the Pareto frontier; export as JSON, Python, or a hosted endpoint

## Supported workflow architectures

- Multi-agent committee: multiple LLM agents with hand-offs and cross-talk; preferred when isolation or domain-breadth is required
- Agentic glue + skills: one conductor LLM calling deterministic tools; preferred when cost/latency are in the objective

## Licensing

- Free for research and personal use: PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0
- Commercial use: custom license — contact AutoAW Labs
